description: Customer-facing product, 50+ engineers, $50M+ ARR, regulated (SOC2 / HIPAA / PCI-DSS). Cadence: per-PR with strict gates. Cloud ceiling: $50K+/mo. Engineering org has dedicated platform team.

anti_recommendations.monolith-no-modular-boundaries: kill — review cycle breaks past 30 engineers
anti_recommendations.single-database-no-read-replicas: kill — fan-out reads at this scale collapse the primary
anti_recommendations.no-feature-flags: kill — gates are mandatory at per-PR cadence
anti_recommendations.no-platform-team: warn — dedicate 8-15% of headcount or velocity halves (Team Topologies, Skelton 2019)
anti_recommendations.no-disaster-recovery-runbook: kill — RTO/RPO must be documented and tested

canon_references: Matthew Skelton & Manuel Pais, Team Topologies (2019) — platform team sizing
Sam Newman, Building Microservices 2e (2021) — bounded-context boundaries
Google SRE Workbook — SLO/SLI/error-budget discipline at scale
Susan Fowler, Production-Ready Microservices (2017) — eight pillars
ITIL v4 / Change Advisory Board pattern for regulated industries
